top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer is anchored on the verse's specific double-inversion structure (yā niśā... tasyāṁ jāgarti / yasyāṁ jāgrati... sā niśā): 'what the saṁyamī sees fully, others do not register; what others register fully, the muni does not see' — verbatim mirror of the verse's two clauses. The saṁyamī / muniḥ technical-term pair is invoked explicitly. The 'same scene; redirected aperture' framing is Shankara's structural reading verbatim. No other verse runs this double-inversion.

BG 5.16 (jñānena tu tad ajñānaṁ yeṣāṁ nāśitam ātmanaḥ / teṣām āditya-vat jñānaṁ prakāśayati tat param) shares the awake-to-substrate / asleep-to-substrate axis but uses the sun-illumination-of-knowledge metaphor rather than the day-night inversion. Distant possibility.

Adjacent sthita-prajña block opening — Arjuna asking who is the sthita-prajña. Could superficially fit because the saṁyamī / muniḥ figure is the sthita-prajña, but 2.55 lacks the double-inversion structure that makes 2.69 specifically what it is.
